title: Protect Audit Information and Tools from Unauthorized Access
description: Protect audit information and audit tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

checks.name: Security Event Log ACL Restricts Access
checks.purpose: Verify the Security event log DACL allows read access only to authorized users (Administrators, SYSTEM, Event Log Readers group) and denies write/delete to standard users

checks.name: Audit Log Clearing is Restricted and Audited
checks.purpose: Confirm auditing of audit policy changes is enabled so log clearing events (Event ID 1102) are captured and generate an alert

checks.name: Audit Tools and Configuration Files are Protected
checks.purpose: Verify auditpol.exe and Group Policy audit settings are not modifiable by standard users

checks.name: Security Event Log ACL Restricts Access
checks.purpose: Verify the Security event log DACL allows read access only to authorized users (Administrators, SYSTEM, Event Log Readers group) and denies write/delete to standard users

checks.name: Audit Log Clearing is Restricted and Audited
checks.purpose: Confirm auditing of audit policy changes is enabled so log clearing events (Event ID 1102) are captured and generate an alert

checks.name: Audit Tools and Configuration Files are Protected
checks.purpose: Verify auditpol.exe and Group Policy audit settings are not modifiable by standard users or non-admin service accounts

checks.name: Remote Log Storage is Access-Controlled
checks.purpose: Confirm centralized log storage (SIEM, log share) has access controls that prevent non-authorized users from modifying or deleting log data

checks.name: Audit Log File Permissions are Restrictive
checks.purpose: Verify /var/log/audit/audit.log is owned by root and has permissions 600 or 640, preventing standard users from reading or modifying logs

checks.name: Audit Configuration Files are Protected
checks.purpose: Confirm /etc/audit/auditd.conf and /etc/audit/rules.d/ are owned by root with permissions 640 or more restrictive

checks.name: Audit Binaries Have Not Been Tampered With
checks.purpose: Verify integrity of auditd, auditctl, ausearch, and aureport binaries using package manager verification (rpm -V or dpkg --verify)

checks.name: Audit Rules Prevent Modification of Audit Logs
checks.purpose: Confirm auditd rules include watches on /etc/audit/ and /var/log/audit/ to detect unauthorized changes to audit configuration and logs

checks.name: Audit Log File Permissions are Restrictive
checks.purpose: Verify /var/log/audit/audit.log is owned by root and has permissions 600 or 640, preventing standard users from reading or modifying logs

checks.name: Audit Configuration Files are Protected
checks.purpose: Confirm /etc/audit/auditd.conf and /etc/audit/rules.d/ are owned by root with permissions 640 or more restrictive

checks.name: Audit Binaries Have Not Been Tampered With
checks.purpose: Verify integrity of auditd, auditctl, ausearch, and aureport binaries using dpkg --verify

checks.name: Audit Rules Prevent Modification of Audit Logs
checks.purpose: Confirm auditd rules include watches on /etc/audit/ and /var/log/audit/ to detect unauthorized changes to audit configuration and logs

remediation: Restrict Security event log ACLs to Administrators and Event Log Readers. Enable audit policy change auditing to detect log clearing. On Linux, set /var/log/audit/ permissions to 700/600 and protect /etc/audit/ with 750/640. Add auditd self-watch rules. Verify binary integrity via package manager.
